title: Zevulon Veers
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}}
{{Update|[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]}}
{{Character

Therefore, the latest [[Standard year/Legends|year]] he could have been [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] in was [[9 BBY/Legends|9 BBY]].</ref>

{{Quote|Wedge, we're doing it! We're going to bring down the Empire!|Zevulon Veers, during the Battle of the Emperor's Citadel|audio=ZevVeers2-DEII.flac|<ref name="DEIIaudio" />}}
'''Zevulon Veers''', called '''Zev''' for short, was the eldest [[Human/Legends|Human]] son of [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] [[General/Legends|General]] [[Maximilian Veers/Legends|Maximilian Veers]] and a defector to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. Veers lost [[Maximilian Veers's wife|his mother]] at a young age, causing him to be strictly raised as a loyal [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] youth by his [[Parent/Legends|father]]. The young Veers soon grew distant from his father and began questioning the Empire's motives, viewing their practice of [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]] against [[Alien/Legends|non-Humans]] as immoral. Veers soon joined a [[Battalion/Legends|battalion]] of [[Commission for the Preservation of the New Order/Legends|COMPNOR's]] [[Sub-Adult Group/Legends|Sub-Adult Group]] where he received low scores from a [[Political Reliability Observer]]. After passing junior [[Imperial officer/Legends|officer]] training, Veers was forced by his father to join COMPNOR's elite [[CompForce/Legends|CompForce]] unit. It was there that he came to his decision to defect. While Veers's superior [[Officer/Legends|officer]], [[Ivo Laibach]], was interrogating a [[Unidentified librarian|captive]] suspected of working for the Rebellion, a Rebel [[Task force/Legends|task force]] assaulted the CompForcers and took Veers [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoner]]. After a short [[Time/Legends|time]], Veers defected to the Alliance.

Veers found life as a Rebel liberating, no longer having to be forced down a path of Imperial service by his father. In [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], he participated in the [[Battle of Calamari]] where he served as a [[Gunner/Legends|gunner]] aboard the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] ''[[Emancipator]]''. Veers managed to destroy the [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] [[Allegiance (Allegiance-class)|''Allegiance'']] during the battle, but was forced to abandon [[Starship/Legends|ship]] with the rest of the crew after the ''Emancipator'' began disintegrating inside the [[molecular furnace]] of a [[World Devastator]]. He later took part in an [[Battle of the Emperor's Citadel|attack]] on the [[Emperor's Citadel]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Byss/Legends|Byss]], where he and a New Republic [[Commando/Legends|commando]] team stowed away aboard several [[X-1 Viper]] [[Battle droid/Legends|war droids]] bound for the planet. They launched their attack and made it to the front of the Citadel, but were fended off by a group of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[chrysalide]] beasts. Veers and the surviving members of the commando team were rescued by the [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] [[Salla Zend]] and [[Shug Ninx]], and were transported safely off-world.

==Biography==
===Early life and Imperial service===
[[File:Veers.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Maximilian Veers, the father of Zevulon Veers.]]
The [[Human/Legends|Human]] Zevulon Veers was the only son<ref name="NEGC">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> of [[Maximilian Veers/Legends|Maximilian Veers]], an up and coming [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] [[Imperial officer/Legends|officer]].<ref name="DESB" /> [[Reproduction/Legends|Born]] by [[9 BBY/Legends|9 BBY]]<ref name="Birthday" /> into a life of power and prestige, Veers spent a joyful early childhood under [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] rule in the care of [[Maximilian Veers's wife|his mother]].<ref name="DESB" /> Several years after his birth,<ref name="NEGC" /> Veers and his [[Veers family|family]] went on a [[Summer/Legends|summer]] vacation where his [[Parent/Legends|mother]] contracted a rare, fatal disease, and [[Death/Legends|passed away]].<ref name="DESB" /> Veers was then left in the care of his father, committed much time to Imperial service, and raised the young Veers to honor his mother by becoming a loyal Imperial youth.<ref name="NEGC" />

As Veers matured, he began growing increasingly distant from his father and longed for the love of his mother. He attended the finest schools in the Empire and had everything he ever needed, but he soon began to ask questions regarding the nature of Imperial rule. He wondered why his tutors, who were far more intelligent than him, were [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]], and why friends of his father's who spoke out against Imperial policy kept disappearing. He continued asking these questions but received no answers from anyone, including his father. In the face of what he viewed as Imperial atrocities, such as slavery, Veers began to believe that much of what the Empire stood for was a lie.<ref name="DESB" />

At his father's behest, Zevulon Veers reluctantly joined a local [[Battalion/Legends|battalion]] of the Empire's [[Sub-Adult Group/Legends|Sub-Adult Group]], the youth branch of [[Commission for the Preservation of the New Order/Legends|COMPNOR]]. The idea of working to improve society appealed to Veers, but he did not get along with many of the other youths and saw no point to the constant marching and climbing while singing [[New Order/Legends|New Order]] anthems he was drilled to do. Veers was able to get through his adolescence without making any major mistakes during his time in the Sub-Adult Group, but his father was dismayed at the low marks he received from a [[Political Reliability Observer]]. He was given a lecture by his father on how his low marks and constant questions while at a field trip to a slave camp had the potential to jeopardize the [[General/Legends|general's]] career, causing Veers to keep his ideas to himself from then on. He made it through the next few years without repeating his previous mistakes, but his mistrust of the Empire only continued to grow.<ref name="DESB" />

Veers eventually passed junior [[Officer/Legends|officer]] training with the hopes of being assigned a non-combat position, but his father wished to toughen the younger Veers and forced him to join [[CompForce/Legends|CompForce]], COMPNOR's military branch. Veers thought very little of CompForce and was growing tired of the constant drilling he was being forced to do. A few weeks after the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Imperial victory]] at [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]], in which his father played an important role, Veers and his father returned from a short leave and were both treated as military heroes. Alongside his father, Veers attended numerous celebrations and heard many speeches about the glory of the Empire and the coming defeat of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]].<ref name="DESB" />

===Defection and New Republic service===
{{Quote|Lucky? Sure, I always wanted to crawl into the guts of a war droid, stow away on an Imperial freighter, and sneak onto the Emperor's homeworld. Yippee.|Zevulon Veers|audio=ZevVeers1-DEII.flac|<ref name="DEIIaudio" />}}
During a routine CompForce police operation several months before [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] death at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], Veers served as a [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]] to his battalion [[Commander/Legends|commander]], [[Ivo Laibach]], who began interrogating [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]] suspected of working with the Rebel Alliance. Intending to harden the young CompForcers to the sight of [[Torture/Legends|torture]], Laibach began beating and torturing an [[Unidentified librarian|elderly librarian]], much to Veers's horror. Simultaneously, a [[Squad/Legends|squad]] of Rebels led by the [[Unidentified librarian's daughter|librarian's daughter]] launched an attack on the CompForce battalion and wounded Laibach. Veers, disgusted at what he had witnessed Laibach do to the librarian, freed the old man and healed him with a [[Medpac/Legends|medpac]] just as the Rebel squad leader came to her father's rescue. She was initially distrustful of Veers but was convinced of his loyalty after hearing her father's testimony. Veers was nevertheless taken prisoner by the Rebels but was treated kindly by them.<ref name="DESB" />

Veers soon defected to the Rebel Alliance and served its successor government, the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].<ref name="DESB" /> Upon learning of his son's fate, the elder Veers wished his son had actually died during the Rebel attack and tried to put the shame of his son's defection out of his mind.<ref name="NEGC" /> The younger Veers disowned his father<ref name="SWE">''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> but still hoped that his family would reject the Empire and that his father would understand his decision to defect. However, Veers never saw nor heard from his father again.<ref name="DESB" />

In [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], Emperor Palpatine was resurrected in a [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] body and [[Operation Shadow Hand|launched an offensive]] against the New Republic. Zevulon Veers served as a [[Gunner/Legends|gunner]] aboard the New Republic [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] ''[[Emancipator]]'' when [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]] ordered the ''Emancipator'' and its [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] to engage the Emperor's newest [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapons]], the [[World Devastator]]s, which were [[Battle of Calamari|attacking]] the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dac/Legends|Calamari]]. The ''Emancipator'', under the command of Generals [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] and [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], intercepted an Imperial transmission originating above Calamari and planned to drop out of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] close to the signal's source. Calrissian ordered Veers to fire all of the Star Destroyer's forward [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]] and [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]] at his command upon arriving above Calamari. Veers fired the ''Emancipator''{{'s}} weapons immediately after the fleet dropped out of hyperspace and was able to destroy the source of the Imperial transmission, the [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] [[Allegiance (Allegiance-class)|''Allegiance'']], while its [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]] were down.<ref name="DE3" />

[[File:VipersCitadel.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Veers and the New Republic team break through Imperial defenses.]]
During this battle, Veers was in direct conflict with his father.<ref name="NEGC" /> After [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]] of [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] were sent from the ''Emancipator'' to combat the World Devastators on the surface of Calamari, one Devastator ascended to low [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] and engaged the Star Destroyer. Calrissian ordered Veers and the other gunners to fire all forward [[Torpedo/Legends|torpedoes]] at the Devastator, but the ''Emancipator'' was caught in one of its large [[Molecular furnace|furnaces]] and began disintegrating, prompting Calrissian to order all hands to abandon [[Starship/Legends|ship]].<ref name="DE3" /> The crew of the ''Emancipator'' fled aboard [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pods]] and were picked up by newly arrived New Republic rescue [[Frigate/Legends|frigates]].<ref name="DE4">[[Dark Empire 4|''Dark Empire'' 4]]</ref>

Sometime later, Veers and a large New Republic [[Task force/Legends|task force]] under the command of Antilles and Calrissian infiltrated a shipment of the Empire's [[X-1 Viper]] Automadon [[Battle droid/Legends|war droids]] aboard the Imperial [[Modular taskforce cruiser|Star Hauler]] [[Reliance (star hauler)|''Reliance'']]. En route to the Imperial [[Fortress world/Legends|fortress world]] [[Byss/Legends|Byss]], the New Republic [[Commando/Legends|commando]] team intended to [[Battle of the Emperor's Citadel|launch an attack]] on the [[Emperor's Citadel]] using the war [[Droid/Legends|droids]]. Calrissian was concerned that the war droids would be difficult to control once their battle programming was activated, but Veers assured him that they had manual override in case anything went wrong. Once the war droids were unloaded onto Byss's [[Imperial Freight Complex]], Veers and the New Republic team activated them and began heading for the Emperor's Citadel. Veers, [[Pilot/Legends|piloting]] one of the war droids, came under heavy fire from Imperial forces and, after encountering and defeating Imperial [[Gunship/Legends|gunships]], was able to make it through the [[City/Legends|city]] to the Emperor's Citadel with the rest of the team.<ref name="DEII4">[[Dark Empire II 4|''Dark Empire II'' 4]]</ref>

Veers and the commandos came across a service [[bridge]] leading to the front of the Citadel. While under continued fire from Imperial forces, Veers and Antilles evacuated the war droids as they pressed their attack on the Citadel. At the same time, Palpatine ordered the release of his [[Chrysalide]] beasts in an attempt to combat the war droids. Veers and Antilles fled and took cover upon witnessing the beasts and allowed the war droids to fight them. The Chrysalide beasts continued their assault until every war droid was destroyed and began going after the New Republic ground team. An exhausted Veers continued to fall back with Antilles as the beasts advanced their approach, until the [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] [[Salla Zend]] and [[Shug Ninx]], in control of the [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] ''[[Starhook Ten]]'', disabled an attacking beast with an ion blast and rescued Veers and Antilles. Veers, injured from the battle, struggled to board the vessel. Another Chrysalide beast attempted to attack the ''Starhook Ten'' but was thwarted.<ref name="DEIIaudio">[[Dark Empire II (audio drama)|''Dark Empire II'' audio drama]]</ref> Once everyone was safely onboard, the ''Starhook Ten'' joined the rest of the newly formed smuggling fleet and fled Byss through hyperspace.<ref name="DEII5">[[Dark Empire II 5|''Dark Empire II'' 5]]</ref>

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|I only hope I can be worthy of the comparison someday.|Zevulon Veers, after being mistaken for Zev Senesca|<ref name="DESB" />}}
[[File:ZevVeersPilot.jpg|thumb|left|Veers pilots a Viper Automadon during the Battle of the Emperor's Citadel.]]
Zevulon Veers had a very turbulent relationship with his father after the death of his mother. He described the summer before his mother's death as the last happy one he truly ever had and, as he grew up, continued to miss the warmth of his mother's smile. Veers began to grow apart from his father as he aged and was disturbed by how much value his father placed on his own reputation. The entire course of Veers's life was based on only what his father wanted him to become.<ref name="DESB" />

Veers was a very curious child and constantly asked questions pertaining to the Empire's treatment of its citizens, much to the annoyance of his father. His willingness to help society and his compassion for non-Human slaves often put him at odds with Imperial ideals. Veers had his faith in the Empire shaken numerous times throughout his childhood. He always wondered why the Empire enslaved non-Humans and [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|bombarded]] planets from orbit, and why committing those acts seemingly brought glory to the Empire. When he joined the Sub-Adult Group, Veers found that he could not fit in with the other youths and spent much of his time playing [[Wegsphere]].<ref name="DESB" />

As an adolescent, taking courses in logic and philosophy only strengthened Veers's views of the Empire and allowed him to read between the lines of Imperial [[Media/Legends|media]] reports and [[Propaganda/Legends|propaganda]] broadcasts. He thought of CompForce as little more than a group of teenagers carrying loaded weapons and often referred to the group as "CompFarce." He promised himself that he would not end up conditioned to simply follow orders like his father, a conviction which grew following his father's victory at Hoth. Veers's animosity toward his father and his reduced faith in the Empire eventually led him to his decision to defect to the Rebel Alliance. He was brought to his breaking point after he witnessed an old man being beaten and tortured by his superior officer. Once he was taken prisoner by the Rebels, he was quick to join their cause.<ref name="DESB" />

As a Rebel, Veers found that he could live the way he wanted to rather than having his entire life predetermined by his father. Due to their similar first names, members of the Rebellion often mistook Veers for [[Zev Senesca/Legends|Zev Senesca]], a Rebel pilot who lost his life during the Battle of Hoth. Veers was always baffled by this notion and strove to become as honorable of a Rebel as Senesca was.<ref name="DESB" /> He made use of his many skills during his time in the Rebellion and its successor state, the New Republic. He served as a gunner in the crew pit aboard the Star Destroyer ''Emancipator'' during the Battle of Calamari, and was able to destroy a Super Star Destroyer with his gunning skills.<ref name="DE3" /> He later piloted an X-1 Viper war droid during the attack on the Emperor's Citadel and had extensive knowledge of the droid's battle programming.<ref name="DEII4" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:FakeVeers.jpg|thumb|right|200px|A crew member aboard the ''Emancipator'' who was mistaken for Veers.]]
Zevulon Veers first appeared in the [[Dark Empire 3|third issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, written by [[Tom Veitch]] and illustrated by [[Cam Kennedy]]. He later appeared in the [[Dark Empire II 4|fourth issue]] of ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire II]]'' and was voiced by [[John Cygan]] in the comic book's [[Dark Empire II (audio drama)|audio adaptation]]. He was given a full backstory in the ''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'' by [[Michael Allen Horne]]. Veers was given additional background information in Maximilian Veers's entry in ''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''.

The ''Dark Empire Sourcebook'' mistakenly uses an image of a different crew member aboard the ''Emancipator'' in its entry for Zevulon Veers.<ref name="DESB" /> It uses the image of a communications officer sitting next to Calrissian and Antilles, despite the fact that Calrissian refers to someone in the crew pit when he speaks to Veers.<ref name="DE3" /> Veers is completely absent from the [[Dark Empire II 5|fifth issue]] of ''Dark Empire II'', although the events of that issue are depicted in the ''Dark Empire II'' audio drama with Veers included.<ref name="DEIIaudio" /><ref name="DEII5" />
